excerpt: | ENCORE: Efficient Architecture Verification Framework with FPGA Acceleration | Verified high level. ACM DOI 10.1145/3543622.3573187 and SIGDA FPGA 2023 TOC describe DUT and software emulator running simultaneously on the same FPGA, with hardware modules monitoring/comparing key registers and pausing on mismatches. | FPGA-accelerated processor architecture verification using a DUT plus reference emulator, dynamic comparison, and mismatch stop. Used for processor verification/debug rather than field-style replay. | Close because it co-executes hardware with a reference model and stops at divergent architectural state, a debugging use case near deterministic replay. | ReplayCapsule-RV should not depend on concurrent golden-model lockstep as its novelty. It aims at recording event-sufficient nondeterminism for replay of a single RV32I embedded failure with interrupts/MMIO, not broad CPU architecture verification. | "First FPGA-accelerated RISC-V verification"; "beats ENCORE"; "full reference-model equivalence"; "coverage improvement" without an evaluation. | "ENCORE validates processor behavior by live differential checking; ReplayCapsule-RV instead packages the external events needed to replay a failure path." |
guidance: Avoid first-ever claims unless a systematic survey supports them.

excerpt: | RISC-V E-Trace | Verified. Official RISC-V Efficient Trace for RISC-V v2.0 is ratified. It defines branch/delta trace, reporting PC discontinuities, branch outcomes, trap transitions, sync, optional timestamp/context/data payloads, and requires the decoder to have the program binary. | Efficient instruction trace for RISC-V: reconstructs instruction flow from a known start address plus encoded discontinuities. It covers jumps, branches, interrupts/exceptions that take traps, synchronization, filtering, timestamping, payload, and data-trace payload options. | Very close because it already traces interrupts/exceptions and can reconstruct the retired instruction stream. | E-Trace is a trace protocol, not by itself a deterministic replay contract. ReplayCapsule-RV's local contract does not store full instruction trace; it stores commit-indexed replay events such as `IRQ_LEVEL`, `MMIO_READ`, and `MMIO_WRITE`. E-Trace does not by itself guarantee MMIO read values, peripheral state, interrupt injection schedule relative to replay execution, or a compact failure capsule sufficient to drive an RV32I replay engine. | "E-Trace cannot handle interrupts"; "ReplayCapsule-RV replaces E-Trace"; "more compressed than E-Trace"; "supports all E-Trace semantics" without implementation. | "ReplayCapsule-RV is complementary to E-Trace: E-Trace reconstructs control flow; ReplayCapsule-RV focuses on replay-driving events, especially MMIO observations and interrupt delivery." |
guidance: Guarantees must be scoped to stated assumptions and generated evidence.

It covers jumps, branches, interrupts/exceptions that take traps, synchronization, filtering, timestamping, payload, and data-trace payload options. | Very close because it already traces interrupts/exceptions and can reconstruct the retired instruction stream. | E-Trace is a trace protocol, not by itself a deterministic replay contract. E-Trace does not by itself guarantee MMIO read values, peripheral state, interrupt injection schedule relative to replay execution, or a compact failure capsule sufficient to drive an RV32I replay engine. | "E-Trace cannot handle interrupts"; "ReplayCapsule-RV replaces E-Trace"; "more compressed than E-Trace"; "supports all E-Trace semantics" without implementation. | "ReplayCapsule-RV is complementary to E-Trace: E-Trace reconstructs control flow; ReplayCapsule-RV focuses on replay-driving events, especially MMIO observations and interrupt delivery." |

excerpt: | ReTrace: VM deterministic replay for trace collection | Verified. Public PDF describes ReTrace as a VMware-based two-step flow: capture minimal nondeterminism during VM execution, then expand during deterministic replay into instruction, memory-reference, or device-event traces. It records/replays guest execution at the IA32 ISA layer and is implemented in software. | Full VM record/replay used to avoid trace distortion and generate detailed traces offline. Logs nondeterminism rather than full traces during capture. | Very close conceptually: record minimal nondeterminism, replay later, expand detail offline. | ReplayCapsule-RV is not VM-based, not IA32, and not full-system virtualization. Its local event model is commit-indexed at a single RV32I hart and defines MMIO reads as replay inputs, MMIO writes as checked observations, and interrupts as boundary events. | "First to log nondeterminism for later trace expansion"; "first deterministic replay"; "same as ReTrace but hardware" without showing what hardware changes. | "ReplayCapsule-RV adapts the minimal-nondeterminism replay idea to a bare-metal/embedded RISC-V interrupt/MMIO boundary rather than a virtual-machine boundary." |
guidance: Avoid first-ever claims unless a systematic survey supports them.

excerpt: | REMU: deterministic replay in FPGA-based emulation | Verified high level. IEEE/Computer Society metadata confirms ICCD 2023 pages 21-29 and DOI 10.1109/ICCD58817.2023.00014; the public REMU toolkit page describes an FPGA-based emulation framework for checkpointing and deterministic replay with bit/cycle-accurate visibility of processors, memory, and peripherals. Full mechanism and results still need paper-level reading before detailed comparison. | FPGA emulation checkpointing plus deterministic replay for debug/visibility, with public materials emphasizing bit-accurate/cycle-accurate visibility across target processors and system components. | Close because it explicitly combines checkpointing and deterministic replay in FPGA-based hardware emulation. | ReplayCapsule-RV should not claim FPGA-emulation replay as new. It is narrower: an embedded RV32I replay capsule for failure reproduction under interrupts and MMIO, intentionally not promising hidden device-state reconstruction or bit/cycle-accurate full-system visibility unless optional cycle fields are enabled and validated. | "First FPGA replay"; "bit/cycle accurate visibility"; "faster than REMU"; "peripheral replay solved" without implementation and evaluation. | "REMU is the closest FPGA replay baseline; ReplayCapsule-RV must be framed as a different replay boundary and artifact, not as the invention of FPGA deterministic replay." |
guidance: Avoid first-ever claims unless a systematic survey supports them.

excerpt: The workspace started empty and was not a git repository; it now has local checkpoint commits and a locked CI reproduction path. excerpt: Embedded firmware failures often depend on interrupt timing and memory-mapped I/O values that are visible to software but absent from ordinary crash reports. ReplayCapsule-RV records commit-indexed event-sufficient capsules for a scoped class of single-hart RV32I interrupt/MMIO bugs, then replays those events in a host-driven RTL harness. The implementation integrates a ReplayCapsule recorder with PicoRV32, compiler-built RV32I firmware images, Verilator record/replay execution, corruption checking, runtime summaries, and mapped FPGA synthesis. The generated artifact reports compiler-backed full RTL replay PASS rows, replay-critical corruptions rejected with zero unexpected accepts, measured cycle/commit and simulator wall-time overhead summaries, and same-target full-core ECP5 mapped overhead for baseline and ReplayCapsule board wrappers. The current prototype prioritizes replay fidelity and auditability over area minimization: it provides a record-side hardware capsule path and host-driven replay harness, but does not yet implement a synthesizable replay-consume datapath.
guidance: Synthesizable replay-consume wording is only allowed in limitation contexts.

excerpt: Embedded firmware failures often depend on interrupt timing and memory-mapped I/O values that are visible to software but absent from ordinary crash reports. ReplayCapsule-RV records commit-indexed event-sufficient capsules for a scoped class of single-hart RV32I interrupt/MMIO bugs, then replays those events in a host-driven RTL harness. The implementation integrates a ReplayCapsule recorder with PicoRV32, compiler-built RV32I firmware images, Verilator record/replay execution, corruption checking, runtime summaries, and mapped FPGA synthesis. The generated artifact reports \rcFullReplayPass/\rcFullReplayTotal\ compiler-backed full RTL replay PASS rows, \rcNegativeReject\ replay-critical corruptions rejected with \rcNegativeUnexpectedAccept\ unexpected accepts and \rcNegativeNA\ not-applicable corruption rows, measured cycle/commit and simulator wall-time overhead summaries, and same-target full-core ECP5 mapped overhead for baseline and ReplayCapsule board wrappers. The current prototype prioritizes replay fidelity and auditability over area minimization: it provides a record-side hardware capsule path and host-driven replay harness, but does not yet implement a synthesizable replay-consume datapath.
guidance: Synthesizable replay-consume wording is only allowed in limitation contexts.

excerpt: ReplayCapsule-RV shows that a scoped class of embedded RV32I interrupt/MMIO failures can be recorded as commit-indexed capsules and replayed in a host-driven RTL harness with compiler-backed firmware. The artifact reports \rcFullReplayPass/\rcFullReplayTotal\ full RTL replay PASS rows, corruption rejection, runtime summaries, and same-target full-core ECP5 mapped overhead. The result is not a replacement for trace standards or snapshots; it is a record-side hardware capsule design and evidence package for replay-driving boundary events. The next step is a synthesizable replay-consume datapath and broader platform modeling, not stronger claims from the current prototype.
guidance: Do not claim replacement for trace/debug standards.

In particular, multicore interleavings, DMA, caches/coherence effects, analog device state, and undefined C behavior are not covered by the current artifact.
